message:
feat(cli): add --project flag to session list for project-based filtering

Adds a `--project` / `-p` flag to `ntm list` that filters tmux sessions
by their base project name, complementing the existing `--tag` filter.

- SessionListInput gains a Project field (bd-3cu02.14).
- newListCmd() wires the new --project cobra flag.
- buildSessionListResponse() receives and applies the project filter.
- Beads: synced issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.12","title":"Update help text and documentation for --label","description":"## Goal
Update help text, command descriptions, and user-facing documentation to explain the --label flag and shared-directory behavior.

## Context
Users discover features through help text and docs. The --label flag needs clear documentation that explains not just HOW to use it but WHY (multiple agent swarms on one codebase) and the shared-directory behavior.

## Changes

### 1. Command help text (automatic via cobra)
Each command with --label has a flag description:
```
--label, -l    Goal label for multi-session support (creates session: project--label)
```
This is set when registering the flag. Verify it reads clearly.

### 2. Spawn command long description
Update spawn's Long description:
```
For running multiple agent swarms on the same project with different goals,
use --label:

  ntm spawn myproject --label frontend --cc=3
  ntm spawn myproject --label backend --cc=2

This creates separate sessions (myproject--frontend, myproject--backend) that
share the same project directory. Agents from all labeled sessions work on
the same codebase and coordinate via Agent Mail file reservations.

Use ntm list --project myproject to see all sessions for a project.
```

### 3. README.md
Add a brief section under spawn docs or "Advanced Usage":

```markdown
### Multi-Session Labels

Run multiple agent swarms on the same project with different goals:

    ntm spawn myproject --label frontend --cc=3
    ntm spawn myproject --label backend --cc=2

Both sessions work in the same project directory (`~/projects/myproject/`).
The label is purely organizational — agents from all labeled sessions see
the same code and coordinate automatically.

Manage labeled sessions as a group:

    ntm list --project myproject     # show all sessions for a project
    ntm send --project myproject "commit changes"  # broadcast to all
    ntm kill --project myproject     # kill all sessions for a project
```

### 4. Key points to emphasize in docs
- Labeled sessions SHARE the project directory (this surprises people)
- Labels are organizational, not isolation boundaries
- Agents from different labeled sessions coordinate normally via Agent Mail
- The `--project` filter lets you manage session groups efficiently
- Session names (with label) are the unique identifier for everything else

- Spawn long description includes labeled session examples

- Documentation emphasizes shared-directory behavior

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.13","title":"Modify GetProjectDir to strip labels via SessionBase","description":"## Goal
Modify GetProjectDir to strip labels from session names, so that labeled sessions resolve to the base project directory.

## Context
This is THE core change of the entire --label feature. GetProjectDir is called from ~35 sites across 19 files. By stripping the label HERE, all call sites automatically get the correct base project directory without any changes.

Current implementation (config.go:2996-2999):
```go
func (c *Config) GetProjectDir(session string) string {
    base := ExpandHome(c.ProjectsBase)
    return filepath.Join(base, session)
}
```

New implementation:
```go
func (c *Config) GetProjectDir(session string) string {
    base := ExpandHome(c.ProjectsBase)
    return filepath.Join(base, SessionBase(session))
}
```

That is a ONE LINE change (adding SessionBase() call). This is why the design is elegant:
- `GetProjectDir("myproject")` -> `~/projects/myproject/` (unchanged)
- `GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend")` -> `~/projects/myproject/` (label stripped)
- `GetProjectDir("myproject--backend")` -> `~/projects/myproject/` (same dir)

## Why This Works

SessionBase() (from label.go) splits on first `--` and returns the base. For session names without `--`, it returns the name unchanged. So:
- Unlabeled sessions: zero behavior change
- Labeled sessions: automatically resolve to the base project dir

All 35 call sites benefit automatically:
- spawn.go: agents work in the correct project dir
- send.go: message context resolves to correct dir
- git/service.go: git operations happen in correct dir
- coordinator.go, dashboard.go, summary.go: all display correct dir
- etc.

## What to verify after this change

Since this is a one-line change in a hot path, verify:
1. `go test ./internal/config/ -run TestGetProjectDir` -- existing tests still pass
2. Add new test: `TestGetProjectDir_WithLabel` -- labeled name returns base dir
3. `go build ./cmd/ntm` -- compiles
4. `go vet ./...` -- no issues

## Acceptance Criteria
- GetProjectDir strips labels via SessionBase()
- Existing tests pass (zero regression)
- New test for labeled session name added
- go build, go vet pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:00:29.643212831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:26.541297704Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:16:26.541282796Z","close_reason":"implemented: label primitives, validation, spawn flag, tests, GetProjectDir","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","label-feature"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.13","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:00:29.643212831Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cu02.13","depends_on_id":"bd-3cu02.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:00:40.230982356Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3cu02.14","title":"Add --project filter to list, send, and kill commands","description":"## Goal
Add `--project` / `-p` filter to ntm list, send, and kill commands for managing groups of labeled sessions.

## Context
When users have multiple labeled sessions for the same project, they need ergonomic ways to target them as a group:
- "Show me all sessions for myproject" -> `ntm list --project myproject`
- "Send a message to all myproject sessions" -> `ntm send --project myproject "message"`
- "Kill all myproject sessions" -> `ntm kill --project myproject`

Without this, users must run separate commands for each labeled session. With 3-5 labeled sessions, this is tedious and error-prone.

## Implementation

### ntm list --project <name>
Filter the session list to only show sessions whose base project name matches:
```go
if projectFilter != "" {
    var filtered []SessionInfo
    for _, s := range sessions {
        if config.SessionBase(s.Name) == projectFilter {
            filtered = append(filtered, s)
        }
    }
    sessions = filtered
}
```

This shows both labeled and unlabeled sessions for the project:
```
$ ntm list --project myproject
SESSION              LABEL      AGENTS  STATUS
myproject            -          2       running
myproject--frontend  frontend   3       running
myproject--backend   backend    2       running
```

### ntm send --project <name> "message"
Broadcast a message to ALL sessions matching the project filter:
```go
if projectFilter != "" {
    var targets []string
    for _, s := range sessions {
        if config.SessionBase(s.Name) == projectFilter {
            targets = append(targets, s.Name)
        }
    }
    for _, target := range targets {
        sendToSession(target, message)
    }
}
```

User experience:
```
$ ntm send --project myproject "please commit your changes"
Sent to 3 sessions: myproject, myproject--frontend, myproject--backend
```

### ntm kill --project <name>
Kill ALL sessions matching the project filter. Requires confirmation since this is destructive:
```
$ ntm kill --project myproject
Kill 3 sessions? myproject, myproject--frontend, myproject--backend [y/N]: y
Killed myproject
Killed myproject--frontend
Killed myproject--backend
```

### Flag registration
Add to each command:
```go
cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&projectFilter, "project", "p", "", "Filter by base project name (targets all labeled sessions)")
```

Note: `-p` is currently unused by list/send/kill commands (verify this first).

### Mutual exclusivity
`--project` and positional session name argument should be mutually exclusive:
```go
if projectFilter != "" && len(args) > 0 {
    return fmt.Errorf("cannot use --project with a specific session name")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--project` flag on list, send, kill commands
- Filters sessions by base project name using SessionBase()
- ntm kill --project requires confirmation
- Mutually exclusive with positional session name
- Tests for filter logic

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.15","title":"Verify checkpoint, Agent Mail, CASS with shared project dirs","description":"## Goal
Verify that checkpoint, Agent Mail, and CASS systems work correctly when multiple labeled sessions share the same project directory.

## Context
With GetProjectDir stripping labels, multiple labeled sessions work in the same directory. We need to verify that session-specific systems (checkpoints, Agent Mail, CASS) remain correctly scoped to each session.

## Systems to Verify

### 1. Checkpoints (internal/checkpoint/)
Checkpoints save and restore session state. Key questions:
- How are checkpoint files named/stored? If keyed by session name (which includes label), no collision.
- If keyed by project dir, multiple sessions would collide.
- Search for: `GetProjectDir` calls in checkpoint package, and how session name is used as key.

Expected: Checkpoints are keyed by session name -> no collision -> no change needed.

### 2. Agent Mail Integration
Agent Mail uses `project_key` for registration. If project_key is the project directory path:
- Multiple labeled sessions register agents in the SAME project space
- This is CORRECT: they're working on the same project, they SHOULD see each other
- File reservations between sessions will correctly prevent conflicts
- Agent identities are unique because they include session/pane context

Verify: What is used as project_key in root.go, spawn.go? Is it GetProjectDir output?

### 3. CASS Context (session history)
CASS stores session history for context injection. Key questions:
- Is CASS context keyed by session name or project dir?
- If by session name: labeled sessions have independent CASS histories -> correct
- If by project dir: labeled sessions share CASS history -> might be desirable actually (shared project knowledge)

Verify: How is CASS context looked up in cli/ commands?

### 4. Git Operations (internal/git/service.go)
git/service.go has 3 GetProjectDir call sites. With label stripping:
- All git operations happen in the base project dir -> correct
- Branch names use session name (unique) -> no collision
- Working directory is shared -> agents see each other's uncommitted changes -> correct behavior per AGENTS.md

### 5. .ntm/ Directory
Some NTM state is stored in `{projectDir}/.ntm/`. If multiple sessions share a project dir:
- `.ntm/worktrees/` - not used (worktrees disabled)
- `.ntm/` other files - check for session-name scoping

4. Add test cases if needed

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.2","title":"Implement ParseSessionLabel and FormatSessionName primitives","description":"## Goal
Implement ParseSessionLabel() and FormatSessionName() as the canonical label parsing primitives. These are the foundation every other change builds on.

## Context
NTM session names will support an optional goal label via `--` separator: `myproject--frontend`. The session name IS the primary key for tmux sessions, project directories, pane names, Agent Mail identity, and CASS context. By embedding the label in the session name, all existing downstream code works automatically.

## Implementation

### File: internal/config/label.go

```go
package config

import "strings"

// ParseSessionLabel splits a session name into base project name and optional label.
// The separator is the FIRST occurrence of "--" (double-dash).
// Returns (base, label) where label is empty if no "--" present.
//
// Examples:
//   ParseSessionLabel("myproject")          -> ("myproject", "")
//   ParseSessionLabel("my-project--frontend") -> ("my-project", "frontend")
//   ParseSessionLabel("foo--bar--baz")      -> ("foo", "bar--baz")
func ParseSessionLabel(sessionName string) (base, label string) {
    idx := strings.Index(sessionName, "--")
    if idx < 0 {
        return sessionName, ""
    }
    return sessionName[:idx], sessionName[idx+2:]
}

// FormatSessionName constructs a labeled session name from base and label.
// If label is empty, returns base unchanged.
func FormatSessionName(base, label string) string {
    if label == "" {
        return base
    }
    return base + "--" + label
}

// HasLabel returns true if the session name contains a label separator.
func HasLabel(sessionName string) bool {
    return strings.Contains(sessionName, "--")
}

// SessionBase returns just the base project name, stripping any label.
// Convenience wrapper around ParseSessionLabel.
func SessionBase(sessionName string) string {
    base, _ := ParseSessionLabel(sessionName)
    return base
}
```

### File: internal/config/label_test.go
Comprehensive table-driven tests covering:
- No label: "myproject" -> ("myproject", "")
- Simple label: "myproject--frontend" -> ("myproject", "frontend")
- Dashes in base: "my-project--frontend" -> ("my-project", "frontend")
- Multiple separators: "foo--bar--baz" -> ("foo", "bar--baz")
- Label with dashes: "proj--my-label" -> ("proj", "my-label")
- Empty base: "--frontend" -> ("", "frontend") -- this is invalid but the parser shouldn't crash
- Empty label: "myproject--" -> ("myproject", "") -- edge case
- FormatSessionName round-trip: FormatSessionName(ParseSessionLabel(x)) == x for all valid inputs
- HasLabel correctness
- SessionBase correctness

## Acceptance Criteria
- All functions implemented in internal/config/label.go
- All tests pass: go test -run TestParseSessionLabel ./internal/config/
- go vet and golangci-lint pass

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.3","title":"Implement ValidateLabel function","description":"## Goal
Add a ValidateLabel() function and update ValidateSessionName() to correctly handle the `--` separator in labeled session names.

## Context
The current session name regex is `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$` in internal/tmux/session.go:29. This ALREADY allows `--` since `-` is permitted and `+` means one-or-more. So `myproject--frontend` already passes validation.

However, we need a separate ValidateLabel() function to enforce label-specific rules, and we should ensure ValidateSessionName still works correctly when the full labeled name is passed.

## Implementation

### File: internal/config/label.go (add to existing file from task 2)

```go
// ValidateLabel checks if a label string is valid.
// Rules:
// - Must start with alphanumeric character
// - Can contain alphanumeric, dash, underscore
// - Must not be empty
// - Must not exceed 50 characters
// - Must not itself contain "--" (the separator)
func ValidateLabel(label string) error {
    if label == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("label must not be empty")
    }
    if len(label) > 50 {
        return fmt.Errorf("label must not exceed 50 characters (got %d)", len(label))
    }
    if strings.Contains(label, "--") {
```

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.4","title":"Add --label flag to ntm spawn command","description":"## Goal
Add the `--label` / `-l` flag to the `ntm spawn` command, which is the primary entry point for creating agent sessions.

## Context
`ntm spawn` is the main command users run to create multi-agent sessions. Currently: `ntm spawn myproject --cc=3`. With this change: `ntm spawn myproject --label frontend --cc=3` creates tmux session `myproject--frontend`, working in the SAME project directory as `myproject`.

Because GetProjectDir strips labels (task .13), the labeled session shares the base project directory. Multiple agent swarms can work on the same codebase with different goals.

## Implementation

### File: internal/cli/spawn.go

1. Add cobra flag in the spawn command setup:
```go
cmd.Flags().StringVarP(&opts.Label, "label", "l", "", "Goal label for multi-session support (creates session: project--label)")
```

2. In the RunE handler, early in the function (BEFORE session name is used anywhere):
```go
if opts.Label != "" {
    if err := config.ValidateLabel(opts.Label); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("invalid label: %w", err)
    }
    sessionName = config.FormatSessionName(sessionName, opts.Label)
}
```

3. The SpawnOptions struct needs a Label field:
```go
type SpawnOptions struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    Label string // Goal label for multi-session support
}
```

### Where exactly to insert the label logic
Look for where `sessionName` is first set from args[0]. The label transform must happen RIGHT AFTER arg parsing, BEFORE any call to GetProjectDir, CreateSession, or FormatPaneName. The label transforms the session name, then everything downstream uses the transformed name.

### What NOT to change in spawn.go
- GetProjectDir is already modified (task .13) to strip labels -- spawn just passes the labeled name
- CreateSession receives the full labeled name as the tmux session identifier -- correct
- FormatPaneName receives the full labeled name -- correct (pane names include session)

### User-facing behavior
```
$ ntm spawn myproject --label frontend --cc=2
Created session: myproject--frontend
  Project dir: ~/projects/myproject          <- SAME as base project
  Agents: 2x Claude Code

$ ntm spawn myproject --label backend --cc=3
Created session: myproject--backend
  Project dir: ~/projects/myproject          <- SAME dir, different session
  Agents: 3x Claude Code

$ ntm list
myproject--frontend  2 agents  running
myproject--backend   3 agents  running
```

Both sessions' agents work in ~/projects/myproject/. They coordinate via Agent Mail file reservations and see each other's code changes in real-time -- exactly like agents within a single session.

### Error cases
- `--label ""` -> error: label must not be empty
- `--label "my--label"` -> error: label must not contain '--'
- `--label "-bad"` -> error: label must start with alphanumeric
- `ntm spawn myproject--frontend` (no flag, but has --): works as before; GetProjectDir strips the label so it still maps to ~/projects/myproject/

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--label` / `-l` flag added to spawn command
- Label validated via ValidateLabel()
- Session name constructed via FormatSessionName()
- Project directory is the BASE project dir (label stripped by GetProjectDir)
- Existing spawns without --label are completely unchanged

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.6","title":"Update ntm list to display label column","description":"## Goal
Update `ntm list` to detect and display labels when labeled sessions exist, with additional columns for project grouping.

## Context
`ntm list` shows all active sessions. With labeled sessions, users need to quickly see which sessions share a base project and what labels distinguish them. Since labeled sessions share a project directory, visual grouping by project name is important.

## Implementation

### File: internal/cli/list.go (or session.go -- wherever list rendering happens)

1. **Parse labels from session names**: Use `config.ParseSessionLabel(s.Name)` on each session
2. **Detect if any labels exist**: Scan all sessions; if any have labels, enable the extra columns
3. **Add PROJECT and LABEL columns**:

Without labels (unchanged):
```
SESSION       AGENTS  STATUS
myproject     3       running
otherproject  2       running
```

With labels present:
```
SESSION              PROJECT       LABEL      AGENTS  STATUS
myproject            myproject     -          2       running
myproject--frontend  myproject     frontend   3       running
myproject--backend   myproject     backend    2       running
otherproject         otherproject  -          1       running
```

Note: All three myproject sessions share the SAME project directory. The PROJECT column makes this relationship visible. Sessions with the same PROJECT value work on the same codebase.

4. **JSON output**: Always include `base_project` and `label` fields in JSON output (even without labels, for API consistency):
```json
{
  "name": "myproject--frontend",
  "base_project": "myproject",
  "label": "frontend",
  "project_dir": "/home/user/projects/myproject",
  ...
}
```

Note: `project_dir` is the SAME for all three myproject sessions. This is by design.

### Design decisions
- The extra columns only appear in text output when at least one session has a label. Avoids visual noise for users who never use labels.
- JSON output always includes the fields for API consistency.
- `-` is used for unlabeled sessions in the LABEL column for visual clarity.
- Sessions are sorted by base project name, then by label, for natural grouping.

### Sorting
Update the sort order to group related sessions:
```go
sort.Slice(sessions, func(i, j int) bool {
    bi, li := config.ParseSessionLabel(sessions[i].Name)
    bj, lj := config.ParseSessionLabel(sessions[j].Name)
    if bi != bj {
        return bi < bj
    }
    // Within same project: unlabeled first, then labels alphabetically
    if li == "" {
        return true
    }
    if lj == "" {
        return false
    }
    return li < lj
})
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm list shows PROJECT and LABEL columns when labeled sessions exist
- ntm list --json includes base_project and label fields always
- ntm list without labeled sessions is visually unchanged
- Sessions sorted by project, then unlabeled-first, then label alphabetically
- Parsing uses config.ParseSessionLabel (single source of truth)

 {"id":"bd-3cu02.8","title":"Handle inferSessionFromCWD disambiguation for shared project dirs","description":"## Goal
Add disambiguation logic to inferSessionFromCWD for when multiple labeled sessions share the same project directory.

## Context
`inferSessionFromCWD` (internal/cli/util.go:172-221) auto-detects the current session based on CWD. It finds the session whose project directory is the longest prefix match.

### The Problem (now real)
With GetProjectDir stripping labels:
- `GetProjectDir("myproject")` -> `~/projects/myproject/`
- `GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend")` -> `~/projects/myproject/`
- `GetProjectDir("myproject--backend")` -> `~/projects/myproject/`

If the user is in `~/projects/myproject/` and all three sessions are running, the longest-prefix-match produces THREE equal-length matches. The current code picks the first one found, which is non-deterministic.

### Solution: Three-tier disambiguation

Modify inferSessionFromCWD to handle ties:

```go
// After collecting all matches with equal-length projectDir:
if len(matches) == 1 {
    return matches[0].Name, "current directory" // unambiguous
}

if len(matches) > 1 {
    // Tier 1: prefer the unlabeled (base) session if it exists
    for _, m := range matches {
        if !config.HasLabel(m.Name) {
            return m.Name, "current directory (base session preferred)"
        }
    }

    // Tier 2: if all are labeled, return error with helpful message
    names := make([]string, len(matches))
    for i, m := range matches {
        names[i] = m.Name
    }
    return "", fmt.Sprintf(
        "multiple sessions share this project directory: %s. "+
        "Specify the session name explicitly.",
        strings.Join(names, ", "),
    )
}
```

### Why this three-tier approach

1. **Single match**: No change from current behavior.
2. **Prefer unlabeled**: If you have `myproject` AND `myproject--frontend`, and you're in the project dir, you probably mean the "main" session. Labeled sessions are the specialized ones -- if you want them, specify the name.
3. **All labeled, no base**: Ambiguous. The user must specify. This is safe and explicit.

### Edge cases to test
- Only unlabeled session: returns it (unchanged from today)
- Only one labeled session: returns it (no ambiguity)
- Unlabeled + one labeled: returns unlabeled (Tier 1)
- Unlabeled + multiple labeled: returns unlabeled (Tier 1)
- Two labeled, no unlabeled: returns error listing both (Tier 2)
- User in subdirectory of project: same logic, prefix match still works
- User in unrelated directory: no match, unchanged behavior

### Implementation details

File: `internal/cli/util.go`

The current code (lines 191-205) collects matches:
```go
for _, s := range sessions {
    projectDir := filepath.Clean(activeCfg.GetProjectDir(s.Name))
    if cwd == projectDir || strings.HasPrefix(cwd, projectDir+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
        if len(projectDir) > bestLen {
            bestName = s.Name
            bestLen = len(projectDir)
        }
    }
}
```

Change to collect ALL matches at the best length, then disambiguate:
```go
var bestLen int
var bestMatches []SessionInfo
for _, s := range sessions {
    projectDir := filepath.Clean(activeCfg.GetProjectDir(s.Name))
    if cwd == projectDir || strings.HasPrefix(cwd, projectDir+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
        dirLen := len(projectDir)
        if dirLen > bestLen {
            bestMatches = []SessionInfo{s}
            bestLen = dirLen
        } else if dirLen == bestLen {
            bestMatches = append(bestMatches, s)
        }
    }
}
// Then apply three-tier disambiguation on bestMatches
```

### Basename fallback (lines 207-220)
The existing basename fallback also needs checking:
```go
base := filepath.Base(cwd)
for _, s := range sessions {
    if s.Name == base {
        matches++
        matchName = s.Name
    }
}
```

This compares the directory basename to session names. Since labeled sessions share a dir, `filepath.Base("~/projects/myproject")` is `myproject`, which matches the unlabeled session name but NOT `myproject--frontend`. This is correct -- the basename fallback naturally prefers the unlabeled session.

## Acceptance Criteria
- inferSessionFromCWD correctly disambiguates when multiple sessions share a project dir
- Unlabeled session preferred when available (Tier 1)
- Clear error message when ambiguous with only labeled sessions (Tier 2)

```go
    cfg := &Config{ProjectsBase: "/home/user/projects"}

    // Unlabeled: unchanged behavior
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/myproject", cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject"))

    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/myproject", cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend"))
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/myproject", cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--backend"))

    // Multiple sessions resolve to SAME directory
    dir1 := cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject")
    dir2 := cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--frontend")
    dir3 := cfg.GetProjectDir("myproject--backend")
    assert.Equal(t, dir1, dir2)
    assert.Equal(t, dir2, dir3)

    // Different projects still resolve to different dirs
    assert.NotEqual(t, cfg.GetProjectDir("project-a"), cfg.GetProjectDir("project-b"))

    // Dashes in project name preserved
    assert.Equal(t, "/home/user/projects/my-project", cfg.GetProjectDir("my-project--label"))
}
```

Invoke caam CLI to switch to the next available account for a given provider, handling OAuth token refresh and account availability.

## Implementation Details

```go
// CaamClient wraps caam CLI invocations
type CaamClient struct {
    binaryPath string
    timeout    time.Duration
    logger     *slog.Logger
}

// CaamAccount represents an account from caam
type CaamAccount struct {
    ID        string    `json:"id"`
    Provider  string    `json:"provider"`
    Email     string    `json:"email"`
    IsActive  bool      `json:"is_active"`
    LastUsed  time.Time `json:"last_used"`
    RateLimit *CaamRateLimit `json:"rate_limit,omitempty"`
}

type CaamRateLimit struct {
    Limited   bool      `json:"limited"`
    ResetsAt  time.Time `json:"resets_at"`
}

// SwitchToNext switches to the next available account for a provider
func (c *CaamClient) SwitchToNext(ctx context.Context, provider string) (*CaamAccount, error) {
    c.logger.Info("invoking caam switch",
        "provider", provider,
        "binary", c.binaryPath)
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.timeout)
    defer cancel()
    
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, c.binaryPath, "switch", provider, "--next", "--json")
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
            c.logger.Error("caam switch failed",
                "provider", provider,
                "exit_code", exitErr.ExitCode(),
                "stderr", string(exitErr.Stderr))
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("caam exit %d: %s", exitErr.ExitCode(), exitErr.Stderr)
        }
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("exec caam: %w", err)
    }
    
    var account CaamAccount
    if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &account); err != nil {
        c.logger.Error("failed to parse caam output",
            "output", string(output),
            "error", err)
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse caam output: %w", err)
    }
    
    c.logger.Info("caam switch successful",
        "provider", provider,
        "account_id", account.ID,
        "email", account.Email)
    
    return &account, nil
}

// ListAvailable returns all non-limited accounts for a provider
func (c *CaamClient) ListAvailable(ctx context.Context, provider string) ([]CaamAccount, error) {
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, c.binaryPath, "list", provider, "--available", "--json")
    // ... similar implementation
}
```
